# 🛡️ GitHub Branch Protection Setup\n\nThis directory contains enterprise-grade branch protection configuration for TraffBoard, implementing 2025 security best practices using GitHub's rulesets feature.\n\n## 📋 Quick Start\n\n### Automated Setup (Recommended)\n\n```bash\n# 1. Set your GitHub Personal Access Token\nexport GITHUB_TOKEN=\"your_token_here\"\n\n# 2. Run the automated setup\nnode .github/branch-protection/setup-branch-protection.js\n\n# Or with repository parameters\nnode .github/branch-protection/setup-branch-protection.js owner repo-name\n\n# Preview changes without applying\nnode .github/branch-protection/setup-branch-protection.js --dry-run\n\n# Replace existing rulesets\nnode .github/branch-protection/setup-branch-protection.js --force\n```\n\n### Manual Setup\n\n1. **Navigate to Repository Settings**\n   - Go to `https://github.com/[owner]/[repo]/settings/rules`\n   - Click \"New repository rule\"\n\n2. **Import Configuration**\n   - Use the configurations in `rulesets-config.json`\n   - Follow the step-by-step guide below\n\n## 🔧 Configuration Files\n\n| File | Purpose |\n|------|----------|\n| `BRANCH_PROTECTION_STRATEGY.md` | Comprehensive strategy document |\n| `rulesets-config.json` | GitHub API configuration |\n| `setup-branch-protection.js` | Automated setup script |\n| `README.md` | This documentation |\n\n## 🎯 Protection Levels\n\n### 🔒 **Production Branches** (`main`, `master`)\n- **2 required reviews** with stale review dismissal\n- **All CI/CD checks** must pass\n- **Security scans** required (CodeQL, secret scanning)\n- **Signed commits** required\n- **No force pushes** or deletions\n- **Admin enforcement** (no bypass)\n\n### 🔐 **Integration Branches** (`develop`, `staging`)\n- **1 required review** with stale review dismissal\n- **Core CI/CD checks** must pass\n- **Basic security scans** required\n- **Admin bypass** allowed for emergencies\n\n### 🔓 **Feature Branches** (`feature/*`, `refactor/*`, etc.)\n- **Basic CI/CD checks** required\n- **Flexible merge options**\n- **Developer-friendly** with bypass options\n\n### 🚨 **Release Branches** (`release/*`, `v*`)\n- **Enhanced protection** similar to production\n- **Full security validation** required\n- **Signed commits** mandatory\n\n## 🚀 Required Status Checks\n\nOur branch protection enforces these CI/CD pipeline checks:\n\n### Core Pipeline\n- ✅ `🧪 CI/CD Pipeline / install` - Dependency installation\n- ✅ `🧪 CI/CD Pipeline / security-quality-audit` - Security & linting\n- ✅ `🧪 CI/CD Pipeline / test` - Test execution\n- ✅ `🧪 CI/CD Pipeline / build` - Production build\n- ✅ `🧪 CI/CD Pipeline / ci-success` - Overall success\n\n### Security Checks\n- ✅ `🔐 Security Analysis / codeql-analysis` - Code security analysis\n- ✅ `🔐 Security Analysis / secret-scanning` - Secret detection\n- ✅ `🔐 Security Analysis / advanced-security` - Multi-tool scanning\n- ✅ `🔐 Security Analysis / dependency-review` - Dependency vulnerabilities\n\n## 📝 Manual Implementation Guide\n\n### Step 1: Create Production Ruleset\n\n1. **Repository Settings** → **Rules** → **New repository rule**\n2. **Rule name**: `🔒 Production Protection (Main/Master)`\n3. **Target branches**: `main`, `master`\n4. **Rules to enable**:\n   - ✅ Restrict deletions\n   - ✅ Restrict force pushes\n   - ✅ Require linear history\n   - ✅ Require signed commits\n   - ✅ Require pull request reviews before merging\n     - Required reviews: `2`\n     - Dismiss stale reviews: `✅`\n     - Require code owner review: `✅`\n     - Require review thread resolution: `✅`\n   - ✅ Require status checks to pass\n     - Require up-to-date branches: `✅`\n     - Status checks: [See list above]\n\n### Step 2: Create Integration Ruleset\n\n1. **Repository Settings** → **Rules** → **New repository rule**\n2. **Rule name**: `🔐 Integration Protection (Develop/Staging)`\n3. **Target branches**: `develop`, `staging`, `integration`\n4. **Rules to enable**:\n   - ✅ Restrict deletions\n   - ✅ Restrict force pushes\n   - ✅ Require pull request reviews before merging\n     - Required reviews: `1`\n     - Dismiss stale reviews: `✅`\n   - ✅ Require status checks to pass\n     - Status checks: [Core pipeline only]\n5. **Bypass actors**: Repository admins\n\n### Step 3: Create Feature Ruleset\n\n1. **Repository Settings** → **Rules** → **New repository rule**\n2. **Rule name**: `🔓 Feature Branch Protection`\n3. **Target branches**: `feature/*`, `refactor/*`, `hotfix/*`, `bugfix/*`, `chore/*`\n4. **Rules to enable**:\n   - ✅ Require status checks to pass\n     - Status checks: [Basic pipeline only]\n5. **Bypass actors**: Repository admins and maintainers\n\n### Step 4: Create Release Ruleset\n\n1. **Repository Settings** → **Rules** → **New repository rule**\n2. **Rule name**: `🚨 Release Protection`\n3. **Target branches**: `release/*`, `release-*`, `v*`\n4. **Rules to enable**:\n   - ✅ Restrict deletions\n   - ✅ Restrict force pushes\n   - ✅ Require signed commits\n   - ✅ Require pull request reviews before merging\n     - Required reviews: `2`\n     - Dismiss stale reviews: `✅`\n     - Require code owner review: `✅`\n   - ✅ Require status checks to pass\n     - Status checks: [Full security pipeline]\n\n## 🔑 Required Permissions\n\n### GitHub Personal Access Token\nFor automated setup, create a token with these scopes:\n- ✅ `repo` (Full repository access)\n- ✅ `admin:repo_hook` (Repository hooks)\n- ✅ `security_events` (Security events)\n\n### Repository Permissions\n- **Admin access** required for creating rulesets\n- **Write access** minimum for viewing configurations\n\n## 🛠️ Troubleshooting\n\n### Common Issues\n\n**❌ \"Not enough permissions\"**\n- Ensure GitHub token has `repo` scope\n- Verify admin access to repository\n\n**❌ \"Status check not found\"**\n- Ensure CI/CD workflows are created first\n- Check status check names match exactly\n- Run a test CI/CD pipeline to register checks\n\n**❌ \"Ruleset conflicts\"**\n- Use `--force` flag to replace existing rulesets\n- Manually delete conflicting rules in GitHub UI\n\n**❌ \"API rate limit exceeded\"**\n- Wait for rate limit reset\n- Use authenticated requests (token provided)\n\n### Debug Mode\n\n```bash\n# Check what would be created\nnode setup-branch-protection.js --dry-run\n\n# Verbose output\nDEBUG=1 node setup-branch-protection.js\n\n# Test repository access\ncurl -H \"Authorization: token $GITHUB_TOKEN\" \\\n     https://api.github.com/repos/owner/repo\n```\n\n## 🔄 Maintenance\n\n### Regular Reviews\n- **Monthly**: Review and update status check requirements\n- **Quarterly**: Audit bypass actors and permissions\n- **Annually**: Review and update protection strategy\n\n### Updates\n- Monitor GitHub's branch protection feature updates\n- Update rulesets when new CI/CD checks are added\n- Adjust protection levels based on team feedback\n\n### Rollback\n\n```bash\n# Remove all rulesets (emergency)\ncurl -X DELETE -H \"Authorization: token $GITHUB_TOKEN\" \\\n     https://api.github.com/repos/owner/repo/rulesets/{id}\n\n# Or via GitHub UI\n# Settings → Rules → [Select ruleset] → Delete\n```\n\n## 📚 Resources\n\n- [GitHub Rulesets Documentation](https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/configuring-branches-and-merges-in-your-repository/defining-the-mergeability-of-pull-requests/about-protected-branches)\n- [GitHub API Reference](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/repos/rules)\n- [TraffBoard Security Policy](../../SECURITY.md)\n- [Branch Protection Strategy](./BRANCH_PROTECTION_STRATEGY.md)\n\n## 🆘 Support\n\nFor issues with branch protection setup:\n1. Check the troubleshooting section above\n2. Review GitHub's status page\n3. Contact the DevOps team\n4. Create an issue in this repository\n\n---\n\n**Last Updated**: January 2025  \n**Version**: 1.0.0  \n**Maintainer**: DevOps Team
